headline: Apple vs. Microsoft: Which is better positioned based on leadership?
summary: Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook announced that he will be stepping down effective September 1, transitioning from chief executive to executive chairman. Riley Wealth chief market strategist Art Hogan, and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma compare and contrast the two companies amid the latest AI trends.

headline: Amazon to invest up to $25 billion in Anthropic as part of deal
summary:
STORY: Amazon said Monday that it will invest up to $25 billion in Anthropic.

That's as the AI startup committed to spending more than $100 billion over the next 10 years on Amazon's cloud technologies.

The e-commerce titan will be investing $5 billion now, and an additional $20 billion in the future, subject to certain commercial milestones.

This is in addition to the $8 billion Amazon previously invested in the company.

The deal deepens the two firms' relationship as Anthropic rushes to secure capacity to bolster its models.

In a statement, the Claude creator said it expected to bring in roughly 1 gigawatt of new computing capacity by year-end using Amazon's Trainium chips. 

It ultimately expects to secure up to 5 gigawatts of such capacity. 

Amazon CEO Andy Jassy said in a statement that Anthropic's use of Trainium chips "reflects the progress we've made together on custom silicon."

Amazon shares rose over 2% in extended trading.

content.title: Amazon to invest up to $25 billion in Anthropic as part of deal
content.summary:
STORY: Amazon said Monday that it will invest up to $25 billion in Anthropic.

That's as the AI startup committed to spending more than $100 billion over the next 10 years on Amazon's cloud technologies.

The e-commerce titan will be investing $5 billion now, and an additional $20 billion in the future, subject to certain commercial milestones.

This is in addition to the $8 billion Amazon previously invested in the company.

The deal deepens the two firms' relationship as Anthropic rushes to secure capacity to bolster its models.

In a statement, the Claude creator said it expected to bring in roughly 1 gigawatt of new computing capacity by year-end using Amazon's Trainium chips. 

It ultimately expects to secure up to 5 gigawatts of such capacity. 

Amazon CEO Andy Jassy said in a statement that Anthropic's use of Trainium chips "reflects the progress we've made together on custom silicon."

Amazon shares rose over 2% in extended trading.

headline: NEURA Robotics and Amazon Web Services Enter Strategic Collaboration to Accelerate Physical AI at Scale
summary: METZINGEN, Germany, April 21, 2026--NEURA Robotics (NEURA) and Amazon Web Services (AWS) today announced a strategic agreement to accelerate Physical AI at scale – bringing cognitive robots that can perceive, reason, and act alongside humans from development into global deployment. The collaboration combines NEURA's cognitive robotics platform with AWS's cloud and AI infrastructure to help train, validate, and deploy the next generation of intelligent robots.

content.title: NEURA Robotics and Amazon Web Services Enter Strategic Collaboration to Accelerate Physical AI at Scale
content.summary: METZINGEN, Germany, April 21, 2026--NEURA Robotics (NEURA) and Amazon Web Services (AWS) today announced a strategic agreement to accelerate Physical AI at scale – bringing cognitive robots that can perceive, reason, and act alongside humans from development into global deployment. The collaboration combines NEURA's cognitive robotics platform with AWS's cloud and AI infrastructure to help train, validate, and deploy the next generation of intelligent robots.

headline: Microsoft must face $2.8 billion UK lawsuit over cloud computing licences
summary: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software ‌on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, ‌a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday.  Competition lawyer Maria Luisa Stasi is bringing the case on behalf ​of nearly 60,000 businesses that run Windows Server on rival cloud platforms.  They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because ‌Microsoft charges higher wholesale ⁠prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ⁠than Amazon's AWS or Google Cloud.

content.title: Microsoft must face $2.8 billion UK lawsuit over cloud computing licences
content.summary: Microsoft must face a mass lawsuit alleging it overcharged thousands of British businesses to use Windows Server software ‌on cloud computing services provided by Amazon, Google and Alibaba, ‌a London tribunal ruled on Tuesday.  Competition lawyer Maria Luisa Stasi is bringing the case on behalf ​of nearly 60,000 businesses that run Windows Server on rival cloud platforms.  They argued at a hearing last year that the businesses were overcharged because ‌Microsoft charges higher wholesale ⁠prices for Windows Server than for users of Azure, costs that are passed on to customers and make Azure cheaper ⁠than Amazon's AWS or Google Cloud.

headline: AI startup Anthropic commits $100 billion to Amazon's AWS over next 10 years
summary: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic has agreed to commit more than $100 billion to Amazon's AWS cloud platform over the next 10 years to train and run its Claude chatbot.  Amazon will invest $5 billion immediately as part of the new agreement announced this week by the companies, and up to another $20 billion in the future.  The partnership will allow Anthropic to secure up to 5 gigawatts of Amazon’s Trainium chips to train and power their artificial intelligence models.

content.title: AI startup Anthropic commits $100 billion to Amazon's AWS over next 10 years
content.summary: Artificial intelligence company Anthropic has agreed to commit more than $100 billion to Amazon's AWS cloud platform over the next 10 years to train and run its Claude chatbot.  Amazon will invest $5 billion immediately as part of the new agreement announced this week by the companies, and up to another $20 billion in the future.  The partnership will allow Anthropic to secure up to 5 gigawatts of Amazon’s Trainium chips to train and power their artificial intelligence models.

thumbnail.caption: FILE - Pages from the Anthropic website and the company's logo are displayed on a computer screen in New York on Feb. 26, 2026. (AP Photo/Patrick Sison, File)

headline: Did Amazon Just Wreck All of Hims & Hers Upside Potential?
summary: The GLP-1 weight-loss category exploded into a multi-billion-dollar opportunity that blends medication access with ongoing patient support. This morning, Amazon‘s (NASDAQ:AMZN) One Medical platform rolled out a national program that pairs GLP-1 prescriptions with primary care integration — regular check-ins, lab monitoring, nutrition guidance, and management of related conditions like hypertension and diabetes. This goes ... Did Amazon Just Wreck All of Hims & Hers Upside Potential?

headline: GitLab Collaborates with AWS to Bring Agentic DevSecOps to Enterprise Teams Using Their Existing Amazon Bedrock Accounts and Spend
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 21, 2026--All Remote - GitLab Inc., the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps, today announced a deepened integration with Amazon Web Services (AWS). Joint customers can now route GitLab Duo Agent Platform inference through Amazon Bedrock, powering agentic DevSecOps with the Amazon Bedrock models, IAM policies, and AWS spending commitments they already have in place.

content.title: GitLab Collaborates with AWS to Bring Agentic DevSecOps to Enterprise Teams Using Their Existing Amazon Bedrock Accounts and Spend

headline: Map: Big Tech corporates keep circling LLM makers
summary: Amazon can’t get enough of AI, announcing on Monday a fresh $5 billion investment into Anthropic and a commitment to invest $20 billion more tied to “certain commercial milestones.” In return, Anthropic has also committed to spending $100 billion over the next 10 years on AWS and to purchasing Amazon’s custom AI chips. It’s all become part of a typical corporate investor playbook of flooding into the big three large language model providers, jockeying to get any piece they can of the biggest AI

headline: Amazon’s US$25b Anthropic Deal And What It Could Mean For Shares
summary: Amazon.com (NasdaqGS:AMZN) has agreed to invest up to $25b in Anthropic, tied to immediate funding and future milestones. The companies have confirmed a decade-long AI cloud arrangement, with Anthropic committing to more than $100b of usage on Amazon Web Services. The deal positions Amazon as a core provider of cloud infrastructure, custom chips and deep AI model integration for Anthropic. The agreement arrives as large tech peers compete aggressively to secure long term generative AI...
